title: मृत्युञ्जय कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री मृत्युञ्जय कवचम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
त्र्यम्बकं चन्द्रमौलीशं त्रिनेत्रं नीलकण्ठकम्।
अमृतेशं महादेवं मृत्युञ्जयमहं भजे॥

॥ कवचम् ॥
शिरो मे पातु मृत्युञ्जयो ललाटं त्र्यम्बकेश्वरः।
नेत्रे मे पातु सर्वज्ञः कर्णौ पातु पिनाकधृक्॥ १॥

नासिकां पातु विश्वेशो मुखं पातु सदाशिवः।
जिह्वां पातु गिरीशश्च कण्ठं रक्षतु शूलभृत्॥ २॥

स्कन्धौ मे पातु भगवान् भुजौ रक्षतु शङ्करः।
करौ रक्षतु विश्वात्मा हृदयं पातु शम्भवः॥ ३॥

उदरं पातु गिरिशो नाभिं रक्षतु भैरवः।
कटिं रक्षतु दिग्वासाः सक्थिनी पातु नीललोहितः॥ ४॥

जानुनी पातु विश्वेशो जङ्घे रक्षतु भूर्भुवः।
पादौ रक्षतु मे देवो मृत्युञ्जयः सर्वतो वपुः॥ ५॥

प्राच्यां रक्षतु मां रुद्रः आग्नेय्यां पातु भैरवः।
दक्षिणे यमहा पातु नैऋत्यां पातु ईश्वरः॥ ६॥

पश्चिमे पातु विश्वात्मा वायव्यां नीलकण्ठकः।
उत्तरे पातु गिरिशो ऐशान्यां पातु शूलभृत्॥ ७॥

ऊर्ध्वं रक्षतु सर्वेशो ह्यधो रक्षतु शङ्करः।
एवं दशदिशो रक्षेन्मृत्युञ्जयः सदा मम॥ ८॥

॥ फलश्रुतिः ॥
मृत्युञ्जयकवचं यः पठेद्भक्त्या समाहितः।
सोऽपमृत्युमतिक्रम्य शतवर्षं जीवति ध्रुवम्॥

सर्वरोगप्रशमनं सर्वमृत्युनिवारणम्।
मृत्युञ्जयप्रसादेन दीर्घायुरवाप्नुयात्॥

॥ इति श्री मृत्युञ्जय कवचं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Mrityunjaya Kavacham ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Tryambakam Chandramaulisham Trinetram Nilakanthakam.
Amritesham Mahadevam Mrityunjayam-aham Bhaje.

|| Kavacham ||
Shiro Me Patu Mrityunjayo Lalatam Tryambakeshwarah.
Netre Me Patu Sarvajnah Karnau Patu Pinakadhrik. || 1 ||

Nasikam Patu Vishvesho Mukham Patu Sadashivah.
Jihvam Patu Girishash-cha Kantham Rakshatu Shulabhrit. || 2 ||

Skandhau Me Patu Bhagavan Bhujau Rakshatu Shankarah.
Karau Rakshatu Vishwatma Hridayam Patu Shambhavah. || 3 ||

Udaram Patu Girisho Nabhim Rakshatu Bhairavah.
Katim Rakshatu Digvasah Sakthini Patu Nilalohitah. || 4 ||

Januni Patu Vishwesho Janghe Rakshatu Bhurbhuvah.
Padau Rakshatu Me Devo Mrityunjayah Sarvato Vapuh. || 5 ||

Prachyam Rakshatu Mam Rudrah Agneyyam Patu Bhairavah.
Dakshine Yamaha Patu Nairityam Patu Ishwarah. || 6 ||

Pashchime Patu Vishwatma Vayavyam Nilakanthakah.
Uttare Patu Girisho Aishanyam Patu Shulabhrit. || 7 ||

Urdhvam Rakshatu Sarvesho Hyadho Rakshatu Shankarah.
Evam Dashadisho Rakshen-Mrityunjayah Sada Mama. || 8 ||

|| Phalashrutih ||
Mrityunjaya-kavacham Yah Pathed-bhaktya Samahitah.
So-pamrityu-matikramya Shatavarshan Jivati Dhruvam.

Sarva-roga-prashamanam Sarva-mrityu-nivaranam.
Mrityunjaya-prasadena Dirghayur-avaapnuyat.

|| Iti Shri Mrityunjaya Kavacham Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning: मृत्युञ्जय कवचम् में भगवान शिव के मृत्युञ्जय रूप से शरीर के प्रत्येक अंग और दसों दिशाओं की रक्षा का वर्णन है। शिर की रक्षा मृत्युञ्जय, ललाट की त्र्यम्बकेश्वर, नेत्रों की सर्वज्ञ, कर्णों की पिनाकधारी, मुख की सदाशिव, कण्ठ की शूलधारी, हृदय की शम्भु, उदर की गिरीश, और पादों की देव मृत्युञ्जय करते हैं। दसों दिशाओं में रुद्र, भैरव, ईश्वर, नीलकण्ठ, गिरीश, शूलभृत् आदि रूपों से शिव सदा रक्षा करते हैं। जो भक्तिपूर्वक इसका पाठ करता है वह अपमृत्यु को पार कर सौ वर्ष जीवित रहता है और सर्वरोगशान्ति तथा दीर्घायु प्राप्त करता है।
benefits: Traditionally invoked to establish divine armor of lord mrityunjaya for full-body protection
Traditionally recited for removal of fear of untimely death (apamrityu nivaran)
Invokes longevity, health, and freedom from all diseases
Protects all ten directions through Shiva's various forms
Especially powerful when chanted during Maha Shivaratri and Pradosh
description: Mrityunjaya Kavacham (मृत्युञ्जय कवचम्) is a divine armor prayer for protection dedicated to Lord Shiva. Rooted in Rudrayamala Tantra, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Regular recitation is believed to protect all ten directions through Shiva's various forms, create divine armor of Lord Mrityunjaya for full-body protection, and remove fear of untimely death (apamrityu nivaran). This prayer is traditionally recited on Monday (Somvar) and during Maha Shivaratri and Pradosh to invoke divine grace. At 8 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 8 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Mrityunjaya Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Mrityunjaya Kavacham in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Divine armor stotra of Lord Shiva for protection from untimely death, diseases, and longevity.

seoFaqs.question: How does Mrityunjaya Kavacham differ from the Maha Mrityunjaya Mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Maha Mrityunjaya Mantra is the single Vedic mantra from the Rig Veda (7.59.12), 'Tryambakam yajamahe sugandhim pushtivardhanam', traditionally chanted in repetitions of 11, 108 or 1008. The Mrityunjaya Kavacham is a longer composed hymn that builds an entire protective armour around the Mrityunjaya Mantra: it adds anga-nyasa (verse-by-verse placement of Shiva on parts of the body), dhyana (visualisation of Shiva), digbandhana (binding of the directions), and phala-shruti (statement of fruits). The mantra is the seed; the kavacham is the structured protective practice that grows out of it.

seoFaqs.question: How is the Mrityunjaya Kavacham related to the Mrityunjaya Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Both texts share the same theological centre, Shiva as Mrityunjaya, the conqueror of death, but they differ in form and purpose. The Mrityunjaya Stotram, traditionally attributed to Sage Markandeya (who escaped death by clinging to a Shiva-linga as the god of death approached), is a devotional hymn of praise emphasising bhava and surrender. The Mrityunjaya Kavacham, in contrast, is structured as a protective armour, calling Shiva to guard each anga (limb and faculty). Many devotees practise both: the stotram for emotional connection and devotion, and the kavacham for focused protection during illness, surgery or grave danger.

seoFaqs.question: When is Mrityunjaya Kavacham traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Mondays are the universal Shiva days, and the early morning brahma muhurta is the most auspicious time. Pradosha (the twilight on the thirteenth tithi of each fortnight), Maha Shivaratri, the entire month of Sawan (Shravana), and Karthika are intensively associated with Shiva practice. Mrityunjaya Kavacham is also chanted by patients facing serious illness, by their families on their behalf, and during the birth-anniversary star (janma nakshatra) of vulnerable family members. Many priests recommend a 41-day or 108-day sankalpa during prolonged crises, often paired with bilva-leaf offerings to a Shiva-linga.

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Mrityunjaya Kavacham specifically promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The phala-shruti sections of various Mrityunjaya Kavacham editions describe several fruits: protection from untimely death (akala-mrityu), recovery from grave illness, removal of fear and chronic anxiety, neutralisation of planetary afflictions especially during dasha and bhukti periods, and steadiness during surgical and medical procedures. Beyond the immediately protective, the kavacham also promises increase in lifespan (ayur-vridhi), nourishment (pushti), and ultimately moksha through the grace of Mrityunjaya. Devotees commonly pair the kavacham recitation with Rudrabhisheka in Shiva temples or domestic abhisheka with milk, water and bilva leaves.

seoFaqs.question: Should the Mrityunjaya Kavacham be chanted with formal initiation?
seoFaqs.answer: The kavacha format historically belongs to the agama and tantra streams, where mantra-shastra prescribes initiation (diksha) and a guru's transmission for the most powerful practices. In current devotional practice, however, the published Mrityunjaya Kavacham is widely chanted by householders without formal initiation, particularly the Maha Mrityunjaya Kavacham preserved in printed editions accompanied by translation. For deeper sadhana (purascharana with fixed daily counts, homa using the kavacham, or wearing it as an inscribed amulet), initiation from a competent guru is recommended. Beginners can safely start with reverent recitation while building up to more formal practice.
